evidence.quote: the live surface is not a second product or an experiment — it is the MVP product's front end that was never built

observations.why: collapses two things into one undifferentiated body, denying separateness

evidence.quote: these are not two products, they are two surfaces of one system

observations.why: explicit unification claim erasing boundary between surfaces

evidence.quote: are not a separate codebase, they are extensions of a single product

observations.why: same unification move applied to codebase

evidence.quote: I think this is one product, it's not an experiment at the existing MVP product. This is the MVP product, this is the front end that was never built.

observations.why: direct performative unification of product identity

evidence.quote: We're providing a home for perspective and the integration feed

observations.why: we-framing of shared provision, undifferentiated product identity

evidence.quote: We need to define the product value

observations.why: directive obligation framing enacts authority over the collective task

evidence.quote: Simplify.

observations.why: bare imperative, command form

evidence.quote: All notifications and agent messages must be proxies through front.

observations.why: must-language issuing a binding rule

evidence.quote: needs to cache across multiple replies

observations.why: needs-to directive on system behavior

evidence.quote: We need to move commentary, etc to the gutters.

observations.why: directive with obligation modal

evidence.quote: Ask/add note -> always just "send"

observations.why: rule-setting imperative collapsing options to one command

evidence.quote: they should be added and reviewed in the styleguide first and then reused

observations.why: should-language prescribing mandatory sequence/order of review

evidence.quote: Teams does need to jump the queue, for sure.

observations.why: asserts rank-reordering of the integration queue by directive need

evidence.quote: Teams jumps the integration queue

observations.why: queue-jump framing is rank reallocation performed as a title

evidence.quote: Teams integration gates Escher, our actual paying client

observations.why: gating framing ties feature to consequence/value of a paying relationship

evidence.quote: Zoom gates Taylor testing, our most active beta tester

observations.why: same gating-as-consequence structure

evidence.quote: Google meet gates our org's secondary transcription path and the last org suite surface.

observations.why: same gating-as-consequence structure

evidence.quote: Otter and other transcription tools gate individual developer/entrepreneur testing/adoption

observations.why: same gating-as-consequence structure applied to a different actor tier

evidence.quote: SMS is an enterprise/premium and US only feature.

observations.why: tiered feature-to-price/segment mapping

evidence.quote: we need to be in the cents or tens of cents per message

observations.why: explicit cost/consequence threshold framing product possibility

evidence.quote: Maybe that means we can eat user cost as a loss leader in a way that is significant

observations.why: cost/benefit tradeoff explicitly named as strategic lever

evidence.quote: What's required for MVP that our actual clients, i.e. Escher, could use.

observations.why: scoping decided by proportional relevance to paying-client value

evidence.quote: Teams gates Escher, the actual paying client

observations.why: reports the gating-as-consequence pattern applied to Teams specifically

evidence.quote: checks if you can read from the perspective (collaborator) then loads perspective

observations.why: access conditioned on matched reciprocal role/status rather than command

evidence.quote: If you are an admin, collaborator, obviously you can see the whole experience and the perspective. But if you are a new participant who is not a repo collaborator

observations.why: graduated tiers of access matched to role membership

evidence.quote: if you were in the call, you can see the page, and if you click on a node that is linking out beyond the call, you can see it if you have access to that call

observations.why: ratifies an access rule keyed to symmetric membership condition (attendance = access)

evidence.quote: Attendance is the membership primitive; cross-links resolve only with access to the target call

observations.why: reports the attendance-equals-access rule as the resolved model

evidence.quote: either collaborator only or public/explicit share (email or public link)

observations.why: binary matched-condition access scheme, no rank differentiation

evidence.quote: collaborator only + explicit share would hugely simplify

observations.why: reduces access model to a matched pair of conditions

evidence.quote: only send it to people i want to see full depth

observations.why: ratifies a discretionary allocation decision proportional to demo value/consequence

evidence.quote: it should be fast. Maybe Luna?

observations.why: speed/cost tradeoff drives model selection

evidence.quote: the flocking graph viewer and voice profiles are not included. Not included in the product itself.

observations.why: declarative exclusion decision, command over scope

evidence.quote: extract the whole transcript; the memory becomes a generated view

observations.why: ruling delivered as settled architecture decision

evidence.quote: attendance grants the page

observations.why: titles the access rule as reciprocal membership grant

evidence.quote: profiles + flocking + arc as wizard of oz static but interactive page in product skin for demo. v2 roadmapped in product

observations.why: settled scope ruling delivered as fixed decision

evidence.quote: stripped/anonymized/actor-scoped/tiered shares become v2 work

observations.why: reports a scope-deferral ruling

evidence.quote: I really want to be able to message an agent somewhere

observations.why: personal desire statement, no relational model enacted toward audience

mints.name: TwoSurfacesOneSystem
mints.title: these are not two products, they are two surfaces of one system
mints.theme: Collapse apparent separateness into a single undifferentiated whole by declaring it so

mints.name: QueueJumpByNecessity
mints.title: Teams does need to jump the queue, for sure
mints.theme: Reorder priority rank by asserting necessity rather than negotiating turn order

mints.name: GatingAsConsequence
mints.title: X gates Y, our actual [stakeholder]
mints.theme: Frame a dependency as the consequence that unlocks a named stakeholder's value

mints.name: CostThresholdReshapesProduct
mints.title: this product changes a lot if we can get costs down enough
mints.theme: Name a cost threshold as the lever that reshapes what the product can become

mints.name: AttendanceGrantsAccess
mints.title: if you were in the call, you can see the page
mints.theme: Resolve an access question by keying visibility to symmetric membership in the originating event
